title: quercus faginea subsp. lusitanica
intro: Quercus faginea subsp. lusitanica, also known as the Portuguese oak, is a subspecies of the Quercus faginea species, a type of evergreen tree native to the Iberian Peninsula. The Portuguese oak has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Quercus faginea subsp. lusitanica include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and topical creams for various health conditions.
